name: Bombay Monkfish
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Light cream or Single cream
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Place the monkfish in a pan large enough to hold it. Pour milk over and place the pan over moderate heat. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 8 minutes. Turn the fish and cook an additional 7 minutes, or until cooked through. When the monkfish is nearly done, add shrimp and cook for 2-3 more minutes, or until they turn pink. Drain fish and shrimp, discarding milk. Cut the monkfish into bite-size pieces.
1. Beat eggs with tomato paste, curry powder, lemon juice, chopped fresh rosemary, saffron, and half cup cream in a bowl.
2. Mix in fish and shrimp and season to taste with salt and pepper.
3. Turn mixture into 4 individual ramekin dishes and pour an equal amount of the remaining cream over each dish.
4. Bake for 20 minutes or until set. Serve hot with a squeeze of lemon and crusty French bread.
This is an appetizing and stylish way to start a meal. For a light lunch dish for two, cook in one ovenproof dish and serve with a green salad and boiled new potatoes.
